openmeter/billing/creditgrant/service.purpose: Implements creditgrant.Service as a facade orchestrating credit grant creation, retrieval, listing, and external settlement transitions by delegating entirely to charges.Service and creditpurchase.Service. Owns no direct DB access — all persistence flows through the charges pipeline.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() + New()
patterns.description: All dependencies declared in a Config struct; New() calls Config.Validate() before constructing the service and returns an error if any required dependency is nil.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (creditgrant.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid config: %w", err) } ... }

patterns.name: input.Validate() as the first statement in every public method
patterns.description: Every public method calls input.Validate() before any logic and wraps the error with fmt.Errorf before returning.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("invalid input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Delegate to charges.Service — no Ent imports
patterns.description: No openmeter/ent/db import. All mutations go through chargesService.Create, GetByID, or HandleCreditPurchaseExternalPaymentStateTransition.
patterns.example: result, err := s.chargesService.Create(ctx, charges.CreateInput{Namespace: input.Namespace, Intents: charges.ChargeIntents{charges.NewChargeIntent(intent)}})

patterns.name: Ownership check before returning a charge
patterns.description: Get-style methods verify cpCharge.Intent.CustomerID == input.CustomerID and return models.NewGenericNotFoundError on mismatch to prevent cross-tenant leaks.
patterns.example: if cpCharge.Intent.CustomerID != input.CustomerID { return creditpurchase.Charge{}, fmt.Errorf("get charge: %w", models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...)) }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue with HTTP status for business-rule rejections
patterns.description: Business-rule violations (e.g. wrong settlement type) use models.NewValidationIssue with commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute to carry an explicit HTTP status to the encoder.

patterns.name: Private toIntent/toSettlement converters
patterns.description: Mapping from CreateInput to creditpurchase.Intent is isolated in package-private toIntent()/toSettlement() pure field mappers with no business logic.

key_file_guides.role: Single file implementing creditgrant.Service. Wraps charges.Service and creditpurchase.Service; performs customer existence check, ownership assertion, and settlement-type validation before delegating.
key_file_guides.watch_for: toIntent() hardcodes clock.Now() for ServicePeriod/BillingPeriod/FullServicePeriod with a TODO — real period derivation is not yet implemented. After Create(), the service does a second chargesService.GetByID to fetch realizations — a deliberate two-step fetch, not a bug.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db or calling Ent builders directly — all DB access must go through charges.Service or creditpurchase.Service.
Skipping input.Validate() at the top of a new method.
Returning a charge without verifying CustomerID ownership in Get-style methods.
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx.
Adding business logic inside toIntent()/toSettlement() beyond pure field mapping.

decisions.decision: Service delegates entirely to charges.Service rather than holding its own adapter.
decisions.rationale: Credit grants are a specialised view of creditpurchase.Charge; all lifecycle state is already managed by the charges pipeline, so duplicating persistence would create divergent write paths.

decisions.decision: Ownership check done in the service layer, not in charges.Service.
decisions.rationale: charges.Service is namespace-scoped but not customer-scoped; the creditgrant service is the API boundary adding per-customer access control without polluting the generic charges layer.

decisions.decision: charges.NewChargeIntent used instead of struct-literal ChargeIntent{}.
decisions.rationale: The Charge tagged-union has a private discriminator set only by NewCharge[T]/NewChargeIntent; struct-literal construction leaves it zero-valued and accessors error.

code_examples.scenario: Creating a credit grant — path from CreateInput to creditpurchase.Charge
